Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your HR system is secure, accessible, and connected to your payroll, government agencies, and banks. Singapore SMEs must comply with PDPA when handling employee NRIC, salary, and medical data, and you need seamless integration with CPF, IRAS, and MOM submissions. Without strong access controls and mobile access, your HR team wastes time on manual workarounds, and data breaches expose you to regulatory fines and reputational damage.

Core HR.narrative: This is your single source of truth for every employee — their contract, personal details, employment history, and documents all in one place. SMEs need this to onboard new hires quickly, manage offboarding properly (especially for foreign workers), and give employees a self-service portal so they stop emailing HR for payslips and leave balances. Without it, you're managing people in spreadsheets, losing documents, and creating compliance gaps.

Time & Attendance.narrative: This family captures when employees work, manages their leave requests, and calculates overtime and shift pay — critical for retail, F&B, logistics, and field teams where labour costs are your biggest expense. Singapore's Employment Act requires accurate time records, and manual timesheets lead to disputes, payroll errors, and lost productivity. A retail manager scheduling 20 staff across 7 days, or a logistics firm tracking overtime across multiple sites, cannot do this in Excel.

Payroll & Statutory.narrative: This family calculates your monthly payroll, applies CPF and tax deductions, generates payslips, and produces the statutory returns (CPF, IRAS, MOM) that Singapore law requires. One payroll error — a missed CPF contribution or wrong tax code — triggers penalties and employee disputes. SMEs with foreign workers also need work permit tracking and levy management. This is non-negotiable: payroll is your largest HR cost and your highest compliance risk.

Talent & Growth.narrative: This family helps you hire the right people, develop them, and keep them — covering recruitment, performance reviews, training, compensation planning, and succession for key roles. Singapore SMEs face tight labour markets and high turnover; investing in employee development and clear career paths reduces attrition and improves retention. A growing SME that can identify and develop internal talent avoids costly external hiring and builds institutional knowledge.

Lifecycle & Analytics.narrative: This family gives HR leaders and line managers dashboards on headcount, turnover, cost per hire, and other metrics to make data-driven decisions. SMEs benefit from seeing trends — which departments have high turnover, how long it takes to fill roles, what your true cost per hire is — so you can act before problems grow. However, advanced features like AI-driven attrition prediction and workforce forecasting are less critical for smaller teams.
